sections.paragraph:
χειραγωγός, οῦ, ὁ one who leads another by the hand, leader (Plut., Mor. 794d; 1063b; Artem. 1, 48; Ael. Aristid. 45 p. 60 D.; Maximus Tyr. 8, 7h [of God]; Longus 4, 24, 2; Herm. Wr. 7, 2a) Ac 13:11.—M-M. TW. Spicq.

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

Longus Longus , II ?a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1083
